title: A Second Disaster: 15 Years After the Tsunami
subTitle: In a town nearly destroyed by the 2011 tsunami, fishermen who helped rebuild now face a second disaster as climate change and warmer seas threaten their future.

title: A Second Disaster: 15 Years After the Tsunami
subTitle: In a town nearly destroyed by the 2011 tsunami, fishermen who helped rebuild now face a second disaster as climate change and warmer seas threaten their future.

title: Design X Stories
subTitle: Today's theme: Vanishing Tools. As the rising age of artisans puts the survival of certain crafts at risk, we shine a light on the vanishing tools used to keep them alive, and their anonymous designs.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Asia Insight
subTitle: An in-depth look at the realities of Asia today -- a dynamic region which faces political, economic, cultural, and technological change.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Frontrunners
subTitle: Highlighting professionals at the forefront of different fields by showcasing their amazing work, discovering their motivations, and learning more about the challenges they may face in the future.

title: Sumopedia
subTitle: The narrator talks about the ring-entering ceremony in Sumo wrestling. The rope around the rings are believed to be the path of the deity.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Instruments of a Beating Heart
subTitle: First graders in a Tokyo public elementary school are presented with a challenge for the final semester. Examining the Japanese educational system's tenuous balance between self-sacrifice and human growth as it teaches the next generation.

title: Newsroom Tokyo
subTitle: Comprehensive coverage of events in Japan and Asia. It explains the day's developments and what is behind them.

title: Direct Talk
subTitle: Sake brewer Noguchi Naohiko shares his philosophy on balancing ancient tradition with modern innovation, forged over seven decades in the industry.

title: IMPACTS:Climates Change the World
subTitle: Each year, 20 million people are displaced by climate change. If nothing is done, these climate refugees will number 216 million by 2050. We examine footage of problem areas and the latest research.

title: NHK World Japan
subTitle: Summer is winding down, and tonight down by Edo Bay the streets are filled with professional entertainers, food sellers, and revelers. It's getting late, but this party is just getting started.

title: Medical Frontiers
subTitle: Japanese researchers are uncovering more and more of matcha's benefits, from improving sleep and cognitive function to easing depression and even boosting muscle mass.

title: Young Engineers Decommissioning
subTitle: With safety as the top priority, the work to fully decommission the Fukushima Daiichi facility continues, with approximately 3,600 and 5,000 employees engaging in each day's operations.

title: Chatroom Japan

subTitle: Nikki Tsukamoto Kininmonth learns about heat-resistant labels and meets Takehisa Kenji, the CEO of a company that manufactures these labels.

subTitle: Exclusive footage takes you inside Russian-occupied areas of Ukraine. As residents are compelled to change citizenship, and even identity, they face stark choices: to adapt, remain silent, or resist.

subTitle: May J showcases a selection of anisong fan favourites and welcomes MYTH & ROID to the studio for a chat and a live performance.

subTitle: The adventures of Charo, a Japanese dog who finds himself lost in New York City and decides to find his way back home.

subTitle: Explores the growing shift towards a circular economy through the professional repair of electronic circuitry and the creative upcycling of discarded garments.

title: A Cat's-Eye View of Japan
subTitle: Kuro explores the rugged Tojinbo Cliffs in Fukui and heads over to the nearby shopping street. He finds Quu in a lacquerware workshop, who makes her owner's legs fall asleep by sitting on his lap.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Anime Manga Explosion
subTitle: Discovers the secrets of Japanese anime and manga as their popularity continues to grow. Considers the appeal of these Japanese productions internationally, how they're created, and their cultural significance.

title: The New Adventures of Rockie and Her Friends
subTitle: Rockie and friends go into Clattery Woods and help a stranger who claims to be a cactologist move his stuff before learning about his research.

title: I Love 'Mee'
subTitle: Seeing the baby laugh when tickled, Mee thinks everyone likes tickles and happily tickles every child in sight. But Mee's friend Hee suddenly gets angry and says, Stop it! Don't tickle me!

subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

subTitle: A unique walking-eye view of the greatest cities around the globe. Featuring chats with locals and enjoying encounters that only strolling the city streets can bring.

title: Ramen Japan
subTitle: Explores Yamagata's status as Japan's ramen capital, featuring fiery Akayu-style miso topped with a signature spicy paste and delicate Sakata shoyu ramen crafted with handmade noodles and seafood broth.

subTitle: A look at the timeless heart of Japan's ancient capital. Against its rich backdrop of culture and tradition, today's Kyoto continues to innovate and inspire, even after being established 1,200 years ago.

subTitle: Ngoc-san, from Vietnam, wants to help a new coworker sitting next to him and become a great senior, Senpai. To know and understand the proper way, Ngoc-san will tackle a role-play.

title: The New Adventures of Rockie and Her Friends
subTitle: Rockie and friends go into Clattery Woods and help a stranger who claims to be a cactologist move his stuff before learning about his research.

title: Texico
subTitle: Explores how people can improve their analysing skills using visualisation, which makes tasks easy to follow.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: J-MELO
subTitle: May J showcases a selection of anisong fan favourites and welcomes MYTH & ROID to the studio for a chat and a live performance.

title: Little Charo
subTitle: The adventures of Charo, a Japanese dog who finds himself lost in New York City and decides to find his way back home.

title: Japanology Plus Mini
subTitle: Explores the growing shift towards a circular economy through the professional repair of electronic circuitry and the creative upcycling of discarded garments.

title: A Cat's-Eye View of Japan
subTitle: Kuro explores the rugged Tojinbo Cliffs in Fukui and heads over to the nearby shopping street. He finds Quu in a lacquerware workshop, who makes her owner's legs fall asleep by sitting on his lap.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Ramen Japan
subTitle: Explores Yamagata's status as Japan's ramen capital, featuring fiery Akayu-style miso topped with a signature spicy paste and delicate Sakata shoyu ramen crafted with handmade noodles and seafood broth.

title: Core Kyoto Mini
subTitle: A look at the timeless heart of Japan's ancient capital. Against its rich backdrop of culture and tradition, today's Kyoto continues to innovate and inspire, even after being established 1,200 years ago.

title: Easy Japanese for Work
subTitle: Ngoc-san, from Vietnam, wants to help a new coworker sitting next to him and become a great senior, Senpai. To know and understand the proper way, Ngoc-san will tackle a role-play.

title: Zero Waste Life
subTitle: Gardener Yamashita Rikito collects plants with nowhere to go, that must be removed for demolition or renovation, and finds them new homes; saving them while saving those who give them up from regret.
